description: Medium temporal corpus — 25 turns over 8 months with multiple supersessions and opinion evolutions. Tests the temporal detector, supersession surfacing, and opinion-history arc.
persona: David Kim, career changer who moved cities, switched jobs, and changed opinions on multiple topics over time.

turns.content: Hi, I'm David. I'm 34, living in Boston, working as a backend engineer at HubSpot.
Honestly I love HubSpot — the team is great and the codebase is clean.
I just adopted a beagle named Rusty. He's a year old and very bouncy.
Big opinion: Python is the best language for backend work. Django is unbeatable.
I'm vegetarian. Have been since college, about ten years now.
Started getting into rock climbing. Going to MetroRock in Everett twice a week.
Actually I've been reconsidering Python. Working with Go at HubSpot recently and the type system is so much nicer.
Big news — I quit HubSpot today. Joining Stripe in San Francisco starting January.
Moving to San Francisco next week. Found a place in the Mission district.
Settled into San Francisco. The weather here is so much better than Boston.
First week at Stripe — really impressed. The infrastructure code is beautiful.
I'm officially saying it now — Go is my favorite language. Python feels clunky in comparison.
Found a great climbing gym here — Dogpatch Boulders. Switching my routine to climb three times a week.
Update on diet: I'm now vegan, not just vegetarian. Cut out dairy and eggs last month.
Rusty is doing great with the move. Dolores Park is his new favorite spot.
I take back what I said about Python. Just used FastAPI on a side project — it's actually wonderful.
My title at Stripe is staff backend engineer, by the way. Got the level when I joined.
I'm dating someone new — her name is Mei. We met at the climbing gym.
Mei is a product designer at Figma. We've been together about a month now.
Hot take: I now think TypeScript is overrated. Switched my side project away from it.
Rusty turned two this week. Took him to the beach in Pacifica to celebrate.
Mei and I are talking about moving in together. Probably end of summer.
Update on TypeScript opinion — I was wrong. After using it again on a real project, it's clearly worth the syntax overhead.
Bought a road bike last week. Adding cycling to my hobbies — climbing and now cycling.
Mei and I officially moved in together yesterday. Got a place in Bernal Heights.

probes.query: where does the user live currently?
where did the user used to live?
where did the user previously work?
what did the user used to eat?
what was the user's old opinion on Python?
what is the user's current opinion on TypeScript?
what does the user think about Go?
what hobbies does the user have?
what does the user think about cryptocurrency?
